displayTitle: CBSE Class 6 Social Science - Chapter 3 Test 3

questions.en: Which is described as the Indian name of the largest river originating in the Himalayas?

explanation.en: The chapter states that Ganga is the Indian name of the largest river originating in the Himalayas.

questions.en: Which of the following tributaries of the Ganga also originates in the Himalayas?

explanation.en: The chapter states that some tributaries such as the Yamuna and Ghagara originate in the Himalayas.

options.en: Cultivation on slopes by cutting steps into the slope

explanation.en: The chapter explains that cultivation on mountain slopes by cutting steps into the slope is called terrace farming.

questions.en: Why is regular farming difficult in many mountain regions?

options.en: The terrain is rugged and slopes are steep
Mountains have no water at all

explanation.en: The chapter says mountain terrain is usually rugged or rough, with steep slopes, so regular farming is possible mainly in some valleys.

questions.en: Which occupation is often preferred over agriculture in many mountainous regions?

explanation.en: The chapter notes that herding is the preferred occupation over agriculture in many mountainous regions.

questions.en: Which of these is a mountain tourism activity?

explanation.en: The chapter lists skiing, hiking, mountaineering and paragliding as tourism activities in mountains.

questions.en: What can excessive inflow of visitors do to mountain environments?

options.en: Put the fragile mountain environment under pressure

explanation.en: The chapter warns that excessive tourism can put fragile mountain environments under pressure.

questions.en: Who was the first Indian woman to climb Mount Everest?

explanation.en: The chapter states that Bachendri Pal was the first Indian woman to climb Mount Everest in 1984.

options.en: Sudden fall of snow, ice or rocks from a mountainside
A huge wave from the ocean

explanation.en: The chapter defines an avalanche as the sudden fall of snow, ice or rocks from a mountainside.

options.en: Sudden collapse of a mass of earth or rock from a mountainside
A wave caused by an earthquake

explanation.en: The chapter defines a landslide as the sudden collapse of a mass of earth or rock from a mountainside.
